normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation asserts that `weights.len() == values.len()`. Any correctness statement assumes this holds.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  ∀ i : Fin weights.length,
    let w : Nat := (weights[i.1]).toNat
    let v : Nat := values[i.1]
    w > cap ∨ (w ≤ cap ∧ (w ≤ cap) ∧ (w ≤ cap) )
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For each item, the inner loop only runs when `w ≤ cap`; otherwise the item is skipped. This ensures indices like `c - w` are valid when the update is performed.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  let n : Nat := weights.length
  let dp0 : Array Nat := Array.mk (cap+1) 0
  ∀ (k : Nat) (hk : k ≤ n),
    let dp_k := (by
      -- dp_k denotes the dp array after processing items 0..k-1
      admit)
    ∀ c : Nat, c ≤ cap →
      dp_k[c] =
        Nat.max
          (dp_{k-1}[c])
          (if (weights[k-1]).toNat ≤ c then dp_{k-1}[c - (weights[k-1]).toNat] + (values[k-1]) else 0)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing the first `k` items, `dp[c]` equals the optimal value achievable with total weight ≤ `c` using only those `k` items, following the standard 0/1 knapsack recurrence.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  ∀ (i : Nat) (hi : i < weights.length),
    let w : Nat := (weights[i]).toNat
    let v : Nat := values[i]
    ∀ (c : Nat), w ≤ c ∧ c ≤ cap →
      -- During the reverse iteration, dp[c] is updated using the previous-iteration value dp_old[c-w]
      -- not the already-updated dp[c-w].
      (by
        admit)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Because the inner loop iterates `c` in decreasing order from `cap` down to `w`, the update `dp[c] := max(dp[c], dp[c-w] + v)` uses the dp values from before item `i` was processed (the 0/1 constraint).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  ∀ i : Nat, i < weights.length →
    (if (weights[i]).toNat > cap then
      -- processing item i leaves dp unchanged
      (by admit)
    else True)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If an item's weight exceeds `capacity`, the code continues without updating dp; thus dp remains the optimum for the processed items.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  let n : Nat := weights.length
  -- Before processing any items, dp[c] = 0 for all c ≤ cap
  ∀ c : Nat, c ≤ cap →
    (by
      admit : (dp_before)[c] = 0)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Initially dp is a vector of zeros, representing that with zero items the best value is 0 for any capacity.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  let n : Nat := weights.length
  -- After processing all n items, dp[cap] is the optimum for total weight ≤ cap
  (by
    admit : (knapsack_01 weights values capacity) = (dp_after)[cap])
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The function returns `dp[cap]` after all items are processed.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap : Nat := capacity.toNat
  let n : Nat := weights.length
  let W : Fin n → Nat := fun i => (weights[i.1]).toNat
  let V : Fin n → Nat := fun i => values[i.1]
  let best : Nat :=
    Finset.univ.image (fun s : Finset (Fin n) =>
      if s.sum W ≤ cap then s.sum V else 0) |>.sup
  -- dp[cap] equals the maximum total value among subsets with total weight ≤ cap
  (by
    admit : knapsack_01 weights values capacity = best)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Connect the recurrence/invariants to the intended mathematical specification: the maximum total value over all subsets of items whose total weight is at most `capacity`.
